projectDescription: A lightweight three-file protocol kit for maintaining a controlled, operator-approved shared memory layer for recurring Codex best practices across sessions.
keyFeatures: Three-file layered protocol: entry point, runtime store, and compiler
Operator-approval-gated practice promotion — no silent persistence
Explicit authority hierarchy below operator instruction and AGENTS.md
Strict admission and rejection criteria for recurring operational patterns
Compiler-driven practice lifecycle: promote, revise, deprecate, remove
Lightweight design — compiler only loads when explicitly needed
Manual maintenance path with same admission rules enforced
Conflict resolution that always defers to higher-authority instructions
publicApiSurface: shared-best-practice-compiler.txt — practice formation and evaluation layer
Runtime block fields: id (PNNN), status, scope, kind, applies_when, goal, do, avoid
Kind values: environment, orientation, operation, verification
Lifecycle operations: promote, revise, deprecate, remove, reject
Proposal shape: candidate, kind, scope, rationale, proposed block
Authority chain: operator instruction > AGENTS.md > local project instructions > CSBP
